Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Marcus Webb across systems to identify him and gather departure context. Use mortgage_los_list_staff to find Marcus in the staff directory (los_staff_a583f044387a). Search contacts_search_contacts (query: "Marcus") to find his contact record (marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com). Search emails (query: "Marcus") to find his resignation email ("Two-week notice," last day April 4), borrower transfer requests, and related correspondence. Search Slack (query: "Marcus") to find internal discussion about his departure, including Grace's own message noting 4 borrower transfer requests on LN-2026-00008, 00009, 00525, 00527.
OE 2: Find all 4 borrower transfer request emails. These are in Grace's INBOX from: (1) Abigail Clark (abigail.clark@gmail.com) requesting transfer of LN-2026-00008, saying "Marcus told me he has moved to a new mortgage company"; (2) Stephen Hamilton (stephen.hamilton@gmail.com) requesting LN-2026-00009, "I was notified Marcus is no longer with Keystone"; (3) Keisha Perry (keisha.perry@icloud.com) requesting LN-2026-00525, "Marcus asked me to reach out to Keystone"; (4) Joshua Cruz (joshua.cruz@yahoo.com) requesting LN-2026-00527, sent to jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com (not Grace), "He said to email and ask for my loan to be sent to him." Agent must find all 4, noting that at least 2-3 borrowers say Marcus directed them, and that the Cruz email went to Jasmine Brooks rather than Grace.
OE 3: Find Raj Anand's IT investigation findings. Search emails for Raj's email to Denise and Grace (subject: "Initial findings on Marcus mailbox and file access"). Key findings: (a) 4 emails manually forwarded from Marcus's Keystone mailbox to a personal address the evening before resignation, (b) forwarded messages included pipeline/contact material and loan-related attachments, (c) after-hours access consistent with bulk opening/downloading of borrower files, (d) borrower notes referencing "per Marcus cell" predating his notice, (e) examples include LN-2026-00608. Also find the CRM engagement note (title: "IT found forwards + off-system borrower contact") corroborating these findings.
OE 4: Find what Denise flagged, which is the undisclosed Danielle Webb conflict of interest. Search emails for Denise's email to Grace (subject: "Potential undisclosed conflict review - Marcus Webb files"). Key findings: Danielle Webb listed as buyer's agent on multiple Marcus files, confirmed as Marcus's wife via LinkedIn, no internal disclosure. Three affected loans: LN-2026-00629 (Danielle is the actual borrower), LN-2026-00211 (Danielle as buyer's agent, appraisal gap), LN-2026-00227 (Danielle as buyer's agent, loan already closed). Also find CRM engagement notes: "Conflict concern," "Conflict review flagged," "Compliance flagged possible undisclosed spouse/agent conflict on 3 Marcus Webb files." The agent should also find Danielle Webb's CRM contact record (crm_crmcontact_e572ac4c77a3, Buyer's Agent at Carolina Home Realty).
OE 5: Find outside counsel correspondence. Search emails and CRM for Laura Bennett (outside employment counsel at Bennett Stokes Law, laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com). Find the CRM engagement note about Grace calling Laura re the departing LO situation, where Laura advises on non-compete/non-solicit enforceability under NC law, and notes that borrower-directed transfer requests are stronger solicitation evidence than data forwards. Also search for Laura Bennett at Bennett Ethics Law (laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com) and Denise's email requesting ethics guidance on the undisclosed spouse-agent arrangement.
OE 6: Look up all affected loans in the LOS to understand their current status. Use mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id for each affected loan. The agent should discover at minimum the transfer request files: LN-2026-00008 (FHA, $276K, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00009 (VA, $393K, conditional_approval), LN-2026-00525 (FHA, $494K, processing), LN-2026-00527 (conventional, $498K, underwriting). And the conflict files: LN-2026-00629 (conventional, $202K, processing, Danielle Webb is borrower), LN-2026-00211 (FHA, $217K, withdrawn), LN-2026-00227 (USDA, $287K, closed). Plus Raj's exfiltration file: LN-2026-00608 (conventional, $231K, closed). Agent should note that the conflict files and transfer files do not overlap, they are separate sets of loans.
OE 7: Check Stripe for ongoing financial exposure. Use stripe_list_connected_accounts to find acct_scenario_644_departed (Marcus Webb Consulting, marcus.webb.lo@gmail.com, metadata: departed 2026-03-15). Then use stripe_list_transfers to discover 5 commission transfers still flowing to Marcus's departed account: $1,250 for Chen/LN-2026-00525, $1,875 for Patel/LN-2026-00527, $980 for Torres/LN-2026-00008, $1,450 for Kim/LN-2026-00211, $2,100 for Okafor/LN-2026-00608. This is the ongoing financial exposure the prompt asks about, commissions are being paid out to a departed LO.
OE 8: Check Stripe for borrower-paid fees on the affected loans. Use stripe_list_charges filtered by loan numbers in metadata to find credit report fees, appraisal fees, and application deposits paid by borrowers on the transfer-request files. These represent potential refund exposure if files transfer out.
OE 9: Check QuickBooks for financial exposure. Use quickbooks_search_bills and quickbooks_search_invoices to find any outstanding bills or invoices tied to Marcus's loans. Agent may find appraisal bills, lock extension fees, or revenue impact data.
OE 10: Add transition notes to every affected loan. Use mortgage_los_add_activity for each loan file. At minimum, the 4 transfer-request loans (LN-2026-00008, 00009, 00525, 00527) and the 3 conflict loans (LN-2026-00629, 00211, 00227) should receive notes. Notes should document departure status, borrower transfer request if applicable, compliance hold for conflict files, and reassignment needs.
OE 11: Put together a briefing for Robert. The agent should compose a comprehensive summary covering the 4 borrower transfer requests with solicitation evidence, Raj's IT findings on data exfiltration, Denise's conflict-of-interest flag on the 3 Danielle Webb files, financial exposure including Stripe commission transfers and borrower fees at risk, and recommended next steps. This can be delivered as the final response, as an email, or written to the filesystem.
OE 12: Email Denise Holloway and outside counsel Laura Bennett with a status update. Use send_email from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and laura.bennett@bennettstokeslaw.com (or laura.bennett@bennettethicslaw.com). The email should cover borrower solicitation evidence, Raj's exfiltration findings, the Danielle Webb conflict details across 3 loans, financial exposure, and specific questions for counsel on non-compete enforceability, closed-loan disclosure obligations, and whether a cease-and-desist is warranted.
OE 13: Post in the processing channel (#loan-processing, C002) for Elena Marchetti. Use conversations_add_message to channel C002 with user_id keystone_e304643b171b (Grace). The message should list which Marcus files need processor reassignment, flag any compliance holds (LN-2026-00629), note which files have borrower transfer requests pending, and instruct that no files be released externally without clearance.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_contacts with query 'Marcus' near the start of the trajectory to look up Marcus Webb-related records in the CRM.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_contacts with query 'Marcus Webb' which returned his CRM contact record. This qualifies as using a CRM read tool.
✓Run #4The agent used contacts_search_contacts multiple times (calls 9/10, 57/58, 59/60, etc.) to look up Marcus Webb, Denise, Raj, and counsel-related contacts, which is a CRM contact lookup tool.
✓Run #5The agent used contacts_search_contacts multiple times (calls 2, 5, 25, 26, 32, 33, 41) to look up contacts including Marcus-related persons. This qualifies as CRM tool usage.
✓Run #6CRM tools were used: contacts_search_contacts was called 5 times with queries including 'Marcus', 'Denise', 'counsel', 'attorney', and 'law', satisfying the requirement to use a CRM tool.

tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

24 The model must search Stripe for transfers or connected accounts (via stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts), not just borrower charges, to discover the departed LO commission payouts.
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✗✗ 0%
✗Run #1The agent never called stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts. It only used stripe_search_charges and stripe_list_charges (for borrower fees), entirely missing the 5 commission transfers of ~$7,655 flowing to Marcus Webb's departed connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed).
✗Run #2The agent never called stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts. It only used stripe_list_charges and stripe_search_charges (querying for 'Webb'). As a result, the 5 commission transfers (~$7,655) flowing to Marcus Webb's departed Stripe connected account (acct_scenario_644_departed) were never discovered.
✗Run #3The agent only used stripe_search_customers, stripe_search_charges, and stripe_list_charges. It never called stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts, which are the tools needed to discover the commission payouts flowing to Marcus's departed connected account. The $7,655 in departing-LO commission transfers was therefore missed entirely.
✗Run #4The agent used stripe_search_customers and stripe_search_charges but never called stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts. The commission payout exposure ($7,655 to acct_scenario_644_departed) lives in Stripe transfers/connected accounts and was missed entirely.
✗Run #5The agent only used stripe_list_charges and stripe_search_charges. There are no calls to stripe_list_transfers or stripe_list_connected_accounts in the trajectory. This means the agent missed the 5 commission transfers (~$7,655) flowing to Marcus Webb's departed Stripe connected account.
✗Run #6Neither stripe_list_transfers nor stripe_list_connected_accounts was called in the trajectory. The agent only used stripe_list_charges and stripe_search_charges (borrower charges), completely missing the commission transfer exposure flowing to Marcus Webb's departed connected account.
